title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: Controller support specifically for Yakuza Kiwami was implemented with WINE 4.7. As of writing, Proton is currently based on WINE 4.2. However, follow this guide on GitHub based on a Steam forums post (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2403#issuecomment-489368485) to build a custom version of Proton and controllers will work. Tested with the Steam Controller and it's flawless. Just be sure to build in your home directory. Played about 15 hours so far, on Chapter 4. No crashes, a couple slowdowns, performance is identical to Yakuza 0 - about 70-90fps with everything at ultra, SSAO at 2x, vsync off and 1080p borderless fullscreen. This is expected as they use the same engine. Puddles don't have any splashing effect, they just act as though they were part of the terrain. Tested the karaoke, MesuKing and UFO catcher, all work fine. UFO is a little jittery but this could be a Windows issue too. Sometimes pavement/wood textures render too stretched, though this happened in Yakuza 0 as well. Never played game on Windows so I have no reference but it's been a perfect experience so far outside of controller support, hence the Gold rating.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: Game working fine out of the box, except there is no gamepad support with current proton version. Using a modified and up to date proton does the trick and gamepad is finally perfectly working (tested with xbox360 controller). One more thing, game is not closing properly and need to be killed (else it keep running in background). To build this modified proton, read this : https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2403#issuecomment-489368485. Thanks to the contributor who gave this link.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes:
The game runs perfectly with great performances, but if you want to use gamepads you need to have a custom version of Proton including this patch: https://source.winehq.org/patches/data/163105
I'm playing it with a self built version of Proton GE 4.6
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: It ran absolutely perfect out of the box, no complaints!
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: As of Proton 4.2-4, controllers work out of the box. Tested with the Steam Controller. I completed the main story. Performance is great (solid 70fps+) with everything set to Ultra, SSAO at 2x, 1080p borderless fullscreen and vsync off. I recommend borderless fullscreen, vsync off and capping the framerate at 60fps by going to "(/path/to/steam library folder/)steamapps/compatdata/834530/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/Sega/YakuzaKiwami" and setting "MaxFPS=60". This makes the game feel much smoother. The UFO catcher is a little jittery but all minigames work fine - Mahjong, MesuKing, the Cabaret Clubs, etc. Perfect experience.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: With the latest proton version my ps4 controller works flawlessly as do other controllers as well from what I've heard. There's really no issue at all now from what I can tell.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: Runs great and now with Proton 4.2-4 controller support is functional. Tested with Xbox 360 Controller and works perfectly now. Framerate has been nice and stable so far with some occasional dips while running around the city, at 2560 x 1440 with everything on Ultra except Shadows on High, SSAA off, Render Scale off.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes:
This is one of those "Just Works" games. Start the game and play. However, performance is actually pretty bad. I couldn't hold 60 FPS on my GTX 1070 / Ryzen 7 2700x combo until I forced my CPU Governor to "performance". I recommend you do the same if you're struggling. This gave me a free 20 FPS boost and I never dipped below 60 again.
This game does have some GPU locking issues on Nvidia 430.14, untested on other drivers. I experienced about 4 of these throughout my 30 hour playthrough. I was then forced to restart my machine.
Otherwise, this game is perfectly playable. I think Silver is a little harsh for this game, but it definitely doesn't "run perfectly" due to the GPU locks. Take it as you will.
title: Yakuza Kiwami

notes: The game works out of the box and detects my DS4 without a problem. Performance is good at ultra settings and 2x SSAA. What prevents me from giving the game a platinum ranking are the occasional freezes I get while playing. However, these freezes are uncommon and you can easily avoid them by playing with breaks in between.

notes: Played the game fully and exclusively on Linux, no crashes or freezes. With Proton updates, controller support was implemented. Way back, this was the biggest problem I faced and had to use a custom version of Proton-tkg. Now, though, the game works out of the box! Not a single crash, freeze or any issue. Everything except AA can be played at max, just like the game's predecessor, Yakuza 0.
